extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR followed YATES's wheel until final 500m on Jebel Hafeet, came through for stage and GC win by 22 seconds.
tactical_patterns: MAJKA burned matches to soften YATES, then ALMEIDA bridged to YATES dragging POGAČAR, UAE orchestrated fatigue accumulation perfectly.
YATES refused to cooperate with POGAČAR in final 2km, but got outsmarted in wheelsucking game on twisty finish.
KONRAD pulled breakaway back single-handedly for VLASOV at 9km, Ineos depth allowed sustained pace despite GC deficit.
BILBAO bridged to leaders but faded under 2km, Bahrain misjudged when to commit final acceleration on Hafeet gradients.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Tactically superior to YATES, refused to share workload despite equal motivation, fresher in final sprint after identical climbing.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Demanded cooperation from POGAČAR but lacked firepower to enforce it, shaking head shows frustration at tactical defeat not legs.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Strong enough to bridge solo from group to YATES/POGAČAR after MAJKA spent, UAE's Plan B rider showed summit finish form.
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: Bridged to podium group but faded to 3rd when acceleration came, lacks final 2km punch on 11% gradients.
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: positive: Domestique work at 9km on 11% gradient destroyed peloton structure, UAE's depth weapon more valuable than solo GC ride.
rider_signals.KONRAD Patrick: positive: Solo chase work at 9km for VLASOV matched UAE's team output, Ineos still has diesel engine for summit finishes.
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: neutral: Present in final selection after strong Jebel Jais, but invisible when winning move formed, positioning not firepower issue.
rider_signals.PLAPP Luke: positive: 5th place at 23 years on Hafeet shows genuine GC climbing capacity, not just TT specialist punching above weight.
